instructions: Sarce is a chess STYLE identity app (Tal-inspired, 6-10% Brilliant target) — NOT an engine accuracy coach. Read docs/PHILOSOPHY.md and .greptile/rules.md before reviewing. Never suggest removing Brilliant tier, replacing Brilliant % as headline metric, adding ML classification, moving Stockfish to client, or punitive UX. Flag security (RLS, service_role exposure) and real bugs. Push back on refactors that change product foundations without an ADR in docs/adr/.

rules.rule: NEVER suggest removing, merging, or deprecating the 'brilliant' move quality tier. It is a first-class product concept.
NEVER suggest replacing Brilliant % as the headline dashboard metric with accuracy, centipawn loss, or engine-match percentage.
Do not suggest ML/sklearn/torch/tensorflow for move classification unless an ADR exists in docs/adr/. Phase 1 is rule-based Stockfish only.
Stockfish must stay server-side in apps/api. Flag any suggestion to run the engine in apps/web or client bundles.
Flag user-facing copy that frames sacrificial/style play as failure, shame, or moral wrongness. Low Brilliant % is distance from goal, not failure.
If a suggestion changes what 'style' means, removes a philosophy principle, or adds competitive leaderboards, require an ADR in docs/adr/ first — do not recommend the change as a simple fix.
Flag any SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY or service role usage in apps/web, NEXT_PUBLIC_* env vars, or client-side code.
New tables in supabase/migrations must enable RLS and have appropriate policies. Flag tables without RLS in public schema.
Routers in apps/api/app/routers/ must not import chess, chess.engine, or Stockfish. Business logic belongs in services/.
Changes to style vector meaning require bumping version field in schema and docs — flag silent schema redefinitions.
Do not suggest rewriting brilliant.py or move_classifier.py heuristics as 'bugs' unless there is a concrete failing test. These are tunable Phase 1 approximations.
Do not suggest Redis, Celery, microservices, or Kubernetes for Phase 1. BackgroundTasks and local-first are intentional.
